title: Parent to Parent Networking * African American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Parent to Parent Networking * African American Community 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Fathers Foster Parents Perinatal Depression Substance Use Disorder Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE SAWUBONA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally specific services and information to promote optimal health. On site food and child care are available for scheduled events. Direct services include: -Parenting support and educational services aimed at mobilizing Black parents to ensure their children achieve educational excellence -Employment services: development, placement, and support -Family Literacy and STEAM fairs -Cultivating Culture: Gardening and healthy eating program -Advocacy for African American families with young children in need of early intervention/early childhood special education -Parent University classes and experiences focused on: Parental engagement in children's education, effective parenting, and all areas of optimal health (intellectual, physical, emotional, spiritual, socio-economic, and relationships) 901 NE Glisan Street Suite 250 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Classes are usually held Thursday nights. Email (503) 284-8000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE SAWUBONA
